creator: amdmi3@FreeBSD.org
text:
> On the other hand, most people probably use binary packages these days

I highly doubt, but anyway we're speaking of port users here.

> Disabling doxygen by default means that anyone who does need the documentation can't use our binary packages and has to build from source and pay the cost of doxygen and its dependencies.

If a user needs doxygen documentation, he would either have doxygen installed already, or won't have problems with installing it either way.

> Anyone who builds from source doesn't need the documentation can just toss 
OPTIONS_UNSET=DOXYGEN in /etc/make.conf.

Default options are there to suit most users. If most users have to override defaults, the dafaults are incorrect.
